family: Support, options, numerical relations, and local structure
justification: distance from marker via pairwise_distance(player,nearest defender), but 'marker' identity (assignment) needs marking primitive.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Instantaneous 'this player has the ball' state is approximated by nearest-player-to-ball within control distance, but firmly assigning control needs a touch/contact primitive.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Whether the defensive block is set/organized vs scrambling is partly inferable from line_integrity/compactness stability, but 'organized' as a declared state needs a structure-integrity/settling metric (line integrity is itself a typed gap).

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Pass and carry action families can now be distinguished via controlled_line_break_episode/carry_episode/action_chain, but through-gap vs around-gap mode still needs a gap_model.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: transition_anchor scopes the transition and time_to_arrival supports fixed-point reachability, but open-space regions/fields are not generated yet.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Geometry of the rearmost non-pressing players is computable as team_compactness/centroid on that subset, but identifying WHICH players constitute rest-defense needs a role/reference selection that is not a catalog primitive.

family: Match clock, laws, and contextual state
justification: Added-time band at end of a period; clock gives elapsed past nominal period length, but the referee's awarded stoppage amount is an external match-control reference. Gap: awarded stoppage-time reference.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: The start of a runner's movement is detectable from velocity onset, but scoping it as a set-piece run (timed to delivery, attacker role) needs set_piece_structure context.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode anchors the interval and pressure_on_carrier can measure frame-level pressure, but no declared pressure-over-interval/profile aggregation exists.
composition_constraint_note: Requires pressure samples aligned to the carry interval and an aggregation/profile operator.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: transition_anchor and time_to_arrival can evaluate declared outlet points, but denial over a set of outlets requires option-graph and/or cover-shadow semantics.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Reception then forward progression by the receiver is detectable via support_arrival_relation + subsequent forward action, but whether the player can/did turn is fundamentally body-orientation, an unavailable modality.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Final-third phase = in possession with ball in attacking third; possession_segment plus a thirds zoning, but the third boundary needs structured_zones. Calibration: final-third phase partial.

family: Outcomes, continuation, and consequences
justification: within-window mechanic supported and a foul can be an action_event_anchor IF event-marked; but foul/free-kick award is a refereeing adjudication not derivable from tracking. Gap: law adjudication (foul) unless event-supplied.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Coverage/numbers on the far side from the ball is computable via ball_lateral_fraction defining weak side + local_number_relation/count there, but 'balance' as a declared metric and the weak-side region reference need explicit definition.
required_missing_capability: weak_side region reference + balance metric definition

family: Lines, gaps, penetration, and structural breaks
justification: Spacing between two specific ranked lines is the inter-line height delta from multi_line_model (supported as spacing), but as a navigable 'gap' with width/depth semantics it needs gap_model. Calibration: between-lines via multi_line lines = partial.
required_missing_capability: gap_model (for gap semantics beyond line spacing)

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Contested/loose-ball phase distribution needs a contest state; possession_segment distinguishes in/out of possession but a neutral 'contested' state is not an explicit primitive, requiring a contest definition.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: High press = out of possession pressing high up the pitch; pressure_on_carrier + multi_line_model line height + possession_segment give the ingredients, but a press-height phase typing (threshold on where pressure occurs) needs definition.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: Progression phase = in possession advancing through middle third; possession_segment + forward velocity/switch_of_play give advancement, but the zoned middle-third framing needs structured_zones. Calibration: progression phase partial (structured_zones).

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: a passing connection becoming unavailable: change_across_anchor on geometric_progressive_corridor expresses an edge closing, but a true edge (openness) needs time_to_arrival and a graph frame.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: Ball speed over the strike is velocity (displacement-derived), but it must be anchored to a shot event that has no primitive.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Skew (asymmetry to one side) is partially captured by signed_lateral_shift of the centroid vs geometric center, but a declared distributional skew metric of the shape is not a primitive.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: A clearing pass/release away from the box after a set piece is an action event with progression away from goal; controlled_pass_episode + action_event_anchor capture it, but labeling it a defensive 'clearance' needs the set-piece context and a clearance action type.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: Where the delivery lands is observable via relation_destination_entry into a region; but the region must be a structured set-piece target zone (near post / 6-yard / penalty spot) which needs structured_zones / set_piece_structure.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: carry_episode provides endpoint displacement, but integrated path length over a trajectory needs a declared arc-length/profile operator.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: time_to_arrival can estimate arrival to the current ball point, but true player-ball reachability over a moving ball trajectory needs moving-target interception.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Exit = membership falling edge (change_across_anchor) for fixed/corridor regions (supported); dynamic-region exit inherits structured_zones gap.

family: Kinematics and trajectory structure
justification: Velocity vectors of multiple players are available so co-directed/co-timed motion is partially expressible, but a declared synchrony measure (correlated velocity across an entity set) is not a primitive and needs a composition rule over per-entity velocity.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode anchors the on-ball interval and pressure_on_carrier can score pressure, but detecting a direction change/turn requires heading-change or acceleration logic over the carry trajectory.
composition_constraint_note: Requires heading/velocity samples aligned to the carry interval and pressure measured at the change frame.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: An interception = opponent enters the pass lane and takes control mid-pass; pass leg + change of control are supported (controlled_pass_episode + outcome_classification + change_across_anchor), but attributing the interception contact needs a contact/contest primitive; coarse interception partial.

family: Off-ball attacking movement and behavior
justification: displacement of a defender measurable via velocity+pairwise_distance/change_across_anchor; attributing it to an attacker's run (marking causality) is the gap.

family: Off-ball attacking movement and behavior
justification: player making self available; support_arrival + corridor pieces, open-lane clause needs time_to_arrival.

family: Off-ball attacking movement and behavior
justification: arrival at far-post region via relation_destination_entry/support_arrival; 'run' typing to a goalmouth zone is the gap.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: The ball path is reconstructable from velocity/pairwise_distance, but it is meaningful only attached to a shot event, which has no primitive.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Receiving outside the opponent block shape is computable via relative_position_to_line / team_compactness boundary, but defining the opponent 'shape' boundary precisely may need declared composition between compactness + lines.
required_missing_capability: declared composition rule (compactness/line envelope as the shape boundary)

family: Off-ball attacking movement and behavior
justification: movement creating a passing option; support_arrival/offer_window pieces + velocity give the kinematics, but 'to receive' intent ties to passing-lane availability (time_to_arrival) for the open-lane clause.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: An open forward corridor ball->teammate is supported via geometric_progressive_corridor; but a general pass lane as a defensible/interceptable channel needs time_to_arrival and lane-as-defense, which are missing.
required_missing_capability: time_to_arrival (lane safety/interception) for the non-corridor case

family: Temporal logic, set operations, and aggregation
justification: [audit-corrected] Selection over a measured set; expressible via gt/gte ordering over pairwise_distance/velocity outputs; ranking operator. multi_line_model already ranks. Aggregation/argmax operator. — relies on an aggregation/extremum operator absent from the catalog (only gt/gte/lte/eq/neq/persists_for/exists/count_at_least exist).

family: Outcomes, continuation, and consequences
justification: change_across_anchor of a count is supported; but 'options' = available pass options requires reachability/time_to_arrival to define an option, which is missing. Gap: time_to_arrival/reachability.

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Crossing INTO/through a region is supported by relation_destination_entry (ball-enters-region-in-window) and geometric_progressive_corridor for defined corridors, but arbitrary structured-zone crossings need structured_zones.

family: Team shape, units, lines, occupation, and structural change
justification: Whether a line stays straight/intact is partially readable from multi_line_model line fit residual, but a declared straightness/integrity metric beyond rank membership is not a catalog primitive.

family: Outcomes, continuation, and consequences
justification: within-window supported; a shot can be an action_event_anchor IF event-marked, then shot_within = exists(shot anchor) within window. Gap: shot detection itself is event-dependent (no shot primitive derivable from tracking alone).

family: Spatial geometry and topological relations
justification: Convex hull polygon/area is standard geometry from positions and is closely proxied by team_compactness area, but an explicit hull-polygon primitive (for inside/outside tests) is not declared.

family: Phases of play and team behavioral states
justification: transition_anchor can mark loss and team_compactness can measure shape recovery, but a defensive-recovery phase type needs declared shape/termination thresholds.
composition_constraint_note: Must align shape measurements to the post-loss transition window.

family: Reference frames and dynamic football regions
justification: Vertical channels (left/center/right longitudinal bands) are close to attacking lanes which lane_occupancy supports; as static longitudinal bands they are supported, but if defined as half-space dynamic channels they need structured_zones. Treated as fixed bands = mostly supported with a typed gap for half-space variants.

family: Physical action reconstruction
justification: Change of controlling team can be inferred from possession_segment + outcome_classification (turnover), but distinguishing a contested recovery from a clean pass interception needs a duel/contest primitive; coarse recovery partial, contested recovery missing.

family: Match clock, laws, and contextual state
justification: A window keyed on game-state (e.g. score margin / time band); window mechanic + clock supported, but score-state needs a goal/score reference not modeled (goals are not a tracking primitive). Gap: score reference.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: transition_anchor supplies loss/regain anchors, but a reusable duration relation pairing a loss transition to a later regain transition is not yet declared as an authorable capability.
composition_constraint_note: Must pair a possession_loss_anchor with the next compatible possession_gain_anchor for the same team perspective.

family: Support, options, numerical relations, and local structure
justification: unmarked/free player: pairwise_distance to nearest defender large + local_number_relation give a proxy, but 'free' (unmarked) properly needs marking primitive.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Pass completion probability is a learned model, explicitly not implemented.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: A 'set' (ready/balanced) position proxy is fundamentally a body-shape/stance signal; tracking centroid cannot infer set posture. Body shape is an unavailable modality.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Pass completion probability is explicitly called out as a learned model not implemented.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: xG is a learned probabilistic model, explicitly not implemented.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: The build shape from a goal kick is a set-piece/restart structural pattern; restart anchor is supported but team build-up SHAPE typing from the restart needs set_piece_structure and role/reference grouping.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Assigning a defender to mark an attacker is the marking primitive; pairwise_distance gives nearest-opponent distance but no marking-assignment primitive exists.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Cover shadow / passing-lane denial behind a defender is exactly the missing reachability geometry; no cover_shadow or time_to_arrival primitive exists.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode intentionally claims only observed movement-under-control. Counting defenders bypassed by a carry requires a derived defender_bypassed_by_carry capability with its own endpoint/buffer/domain rules.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: A bounce/lay-off (pass to a player who returns it first-time) is the one_touch_relay_episode pattern within an action_chain / pass_chain_episode.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Give-and-go (pass then receive back having moved) is a one_touch_relay_episode / support_arrival_relation pair inside an action_chain; same-possession ordered.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Up-back-through is a three-leg ordered pattern: forward controlled_pass, backward lay-off (one_touch_relay_episode), then a progressive corridor pass; all same-possession in action_chain.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Wall pass = one-two; one_touch_relay_episode with the ball returned first-time, ordered in an action_chain (synonym of give_and_go).
